comment.body: truflation was right, not much inflationary impulse. People under the impression that tariffs have been dropped....but have they. The original tariff on China was 34% +20%....its now 84% +20%. China is about 14% of US imports but more like 40% of us imported manufactured goods....so in effect although they have cut tariffs on the other 60% to 10%....actually the overall tariff on imports is probably something similar to two days ago...maybe slightly smaller

comment.body:
🧭 Refined SPX 0DTE Road Map – Thursday, April 10
Current Spot Reference: ~5350
Straddle Range (5375): ±120 pts → 5255–5495 zone
🔼 Above 5350 (Upside Paths)
IF > 5350 → 5390
→ Top of chop zone, minor friction
IF > 5390 → 5425 → 5450
→ Momentum builds if cleared
IF > 5450 → 5500 → 5525
→ Range extension, moderate resistance
IF > 5525 → 5560 → 5600
→ Higher resistance, possible exhaustion zone
🔽 Below 5350 (Downside Paths)
IF < 5350 → 5330 → 5310
→ Inside chop, small reaction zones
IF < 5310 → 5280 → 5250
→ Opens room for continuation
IF < 5250 → 5225 → 5200
→ Support zones with bounce potential
IF < 5200 → 5175 → 5150
→ Deeper levels, lower band of structure
🌀 Chop Zone (Mean-Reversion Bias)
Range: 5310–5390
→ Expect range behavior, fade edges unless clean break
→ Ideal zone for premium selling and reversion setups
#SPX #0DTE #RoadMap #ChopZone #IntradayLevels #OptionsFlow #TradingPlan #SPXSetup

comment.body: A question I ask myself regarding the alliance between Russia, the US and Saudi Arabia: how do they manage the situation of oil products? In the sense:  in a context of low future growth, trade pressures and possible deflation, the prices of these products will fall, making competition between exporting countries more aggressive. How do you think they will manage it? Already in 2022 there were tensions with the Saudi over the issue of prices that were too low.
